chunk_summary: This section focuses on discharge planning for safe handling, storage, and fortification of expressed and donor milk for home use, emphasizing guidelines for preterm infants and NICU graduates.
learning_objectives: Understand proper storage and handling protocols for expressed and donor milk at home.
Learn fortification techniques to support growth in preterm infants using human milk and formulas.
Recognize the importance of milk banking and donor milk in neonatal care.
Apply evidence-based practices for transitioning NICU graduates to home milk handling.
key_facts: Human milk can be stored in a refrigerator at 4°C for up to 4 days and in a freezer at -18°C for up to 6 months (ABM Protocol #8).
Fortification of human milk is essential for preterm infants to meet their nutritional needs and support growth (PMC10662944).
Donor milk is pasteurized to ensure safety and is often used in NICU settings for vulnerable infants (CPS Position Statement).
Proper handling of expressed milk includes avoiding cross-contamination and maintaining hygiene during preparation (CDC Guidelines).
NICU graduates may require individualized milk fortification plans based on their growth and health status (CPS Position Statement).
reference_quote: Human milk can be stored in a refrigerator at 4°C for up to 4 days and in a freezer at -18°C for up to 6 months. (ABM Clinical Protocol #8)
document_key_topics: Human milk fortification for preterm infants
